heading: Unfunded priorities of the armed forces and combatant commands: annual report

Annual Report on Unfunded Requirements for Laboratory Military Construction Projects, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–91, div. B, title XXVIII, § 2806131 Stat. 1847Pub. L. 116–92, div. B, title XXVIII, § 2807133 Stat. 1885Dec. 12, 2017Dec. 20, 2019“The Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering, in coordination with the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, shall submit to the congressional defense committees [Committees on Armed Services and Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives] each year, at the time the budget of the President for the fiscal year beginning in such year is submitted to Congress under , United States Code, a report listing unfunded requirements on major and minor military construction projects for Department of Defense science and technology laboratories and facilities and test and evaluation facilities, in prioritized order, with specific accounts and program elements identified, and shall include a Department of Defense Form DD1391 for each major and minor military construction project included in the report.”section 1105(a) of title 31

B, title XXVIII, § 2807133 Stat. 1885Dec. 12, 2017Dec. 20, 2019“The Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering, in coordination with the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, shall submit to the congressional defense committees [Committees on Armed Services and Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives] each year, at the time the budget of the President for the fiscal year beginning in such year is submitted to Congress under , United States Code, a report listing unfunded requirements on major and minor military construction projects for Department of Defense science and technology laboratories and facilities and test and evaluation facilities, in prioritized order, with specific accounts and program elements identified, and shall include a Department of Defense Form DD1391 for each major and minor military construction project included in the report.”section 1105(a) of title 31

children.heading: .—Annual Report
children.label: a .—Annual Report
children.text: Not later than 10 days after the date on which the budget of the President for a fiscal year is submitted to Congress pursuant to , each officer specified in subsection (b) shall submit to the Secretary of Defense and the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and to the congressional defense committees, a report on the unfunded priorities of the armed force or forces or combatant command under the jurisdiction or command of such officer.section 1105 of title 31
The Chief of Staff of the Army.
The Chief of Naval Operations.
The Chief of Staff of the Air Force.
The Commandant of the Marine Corps.
The Chief of Space Operations.
The commanders of the combatant commands established under .section 161 of this title
The Chief of the National Guard Bureau in the role assigned to that position in .section 10502(c)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: A summary description of such priority, including the objectives outlined in the national defense strategy required under and the National Military Strategy required under to be advanced if such priority is funded (whether in whole or in part).section 113(g) of this titlesection 139(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The additional amount of funds recommended in connection with the objectives under subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: Line Item Number (LIN) for applicable procurement accounts.

children.label: ii
children.text: Program Element (PE) number for applicable research, development, test, and evaluation accounts.

children.label: iii
children.text: Sub-activity group (SAG) for applicable operation and maintenance accounts.

children.label: D
children.text: A detailed assessment of each specific risk that would be reduced in executing the national defense strategy required under and the National Military Strategy required under if such priority is funded (whether in whole or in part).section 113(g) of this titlesection 139(b) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: The requirement to be addressed by the unfunded priority.

children.label: F
children.text: The reason why funding for the priority was not included in the budget of the President.

children.label: G
children.text: A description of any funding provided for the requirement for the current and preceding fiscal year.

children.label: H
children.text: An assessment of the effect that providing funding for the priority would have on the future-years defense plan.

children.label: A
children.text: In overall order of urgency of priority according to the amount of risk reduced.

children.label: B
children.text: In overall order of urgency of priority among unfunded priorities (other than covered military construction projects).

children.label: C
children.text: In overall order of urgency of priority among covered military construction projects.

children.label: i
children.text: relate to equipping requirements in support of non-federalized National Guard responsibilities for the homeland defense or civil support missions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), were not included in a report under this section submitted by an officer specified in subsection (b)(1) or (3) for any of five fiscal years preceding the fiscal year for which the report is submitted, on behalf of National Guard forces to address a warfighting requirement.

children.label: i
children.text: determines that the inclusion such unfunded priority reasonably supports the priorities of the Department under the national defense strategy under ; andsection 113(g) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: submits to the congressional defense committees written notice of such determination.

children.heading: .—Prioritization
children.label: d .—Prioritization
children.text: Not later than 10 days after the receipt of all of the reports referred to in subsection (a), the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, shall submit to the congressional defense committees a report that prioritizes each specific unfunded priority across all unfunded priorities submitted by officers specified in (b) according to the risk reduced in executing the national defense strategy required under and the National Military Strategy required under .section 113(g) of this titlesection 139(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is not funded in the budget of the President for the fiscal year as submitted to Congress pursuant to ;section 1105 of title 31

children.label: B
children.text: is necessary to fulfill a requirement associated with an operational or contingency plan of a combatant command or other validated requirement; and

children.label: i
children.text: additional resources had been available for the budget to fund the program, activity, or mission requirement; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the program, activity, or mission requirement has emerged since the budget was formulated.

children.label: A
children.text: is included in any fiscal year of the future-years defense program under that is submitted in connection with the budget of the President for the fiscal year, and is executable in the fiscal year; orsection 221 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is considered by the commander of a combatant command referred to in subsection (b)(5) to be an urgent need, and is executable in the fiscal year.
